note:
大流行已经过去十个月了，美国的前景比以往任何时候都要糟糕。全国范围内的冠状病毒病例正飙升至前所未见的水平，仅在周五就报告了近9.7万例的创纪录病例。随着数十个州的医院开始人满为患，卫生专家疲惫地展望未来几周。寒冷的天气和一系列的假期无疑将把人们带到室内和一起，为目前疾病传播的地狱浇上汽油。
然而，尽管现实严峻，唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统和他的政府在总统选举前的最后几天没有解决这场可怕的危机，而是淡化了这场悲剧，攻击公共卫生专家和前线工作人员。特朗普的冠状病毒顾问斯科特·阿特拉斯(Scott Atlas)甚至出现在俄罗斯的一个宣传电视节目中，为政府的反应辩护，在节目中，他呼应了他之前关于疫情的虚假和误导性说法。
周五，特朗普本人谎称医生为了钱而夸大了冠状病毒死亡人数。对此，美国医学会(American Medical Association)主席苏珊·贝利(Susan Bailey)称特朗普的虚假陈述是“恶意的、离谱的、完全被误导的”。
贝利博士在一份声明中说，“我们的领导人应该遵循科学，敦促遵守我们熟知的公共卫生步骤--戴口罩、洗手和练习身体距离，而不是攻击我们，对医生提出毫无根据的指控。”
尽管如此，特朗普的高级顾问杰森·米勒(Jason Miller)在周日接受美国广播公司(ABC)的“本周”(This Week)节目时，加倍强调了这一虚假声明，毫无根据地暗示，“有各种独立的东西表明了这一点。”
在周日深夜的集会上，特朗普继续表示，他将在选举后解雇美国顶级传染病专家安东尼·福奇(Anthony Fuci)。这一评论是在特朗普多次抱怨美国正在肆虐的公共卫生危机得到超过应有的新闻报道后发表的。这促使他的听众开始高呼“火焰杯！火福奇。“。
“不要告诉任何人，”特朗普回应道，“但让我等到选举结束后再说。我很感谢你的建议。“。
在他的后期竞选活动中，特朗普举行了(大多是不戴面具的)集会，并吹捧他的政府已经成功应对了这一流行病的想法。他一再错误地宣称，美国正在“扭转”这场流行病。
“我们会受到很大的伤害。这不是一个好的情况。“福奇在周五晚些时候接受华盛顿邮报采访时说。“当你进入秋冬季节时，所有的星星都排列在错误的地方，人们聚集在家里的室内。你的定位不可能比这更糟糕了。“。
在采访中，福奇还抨击了斯科特·阿特拉斯(Scott Atlas)，他是特朗普最信任的冠状病毒危机顾问，尽管他没有传染病背景。
福奇在接受“华盛顿邮报”采访时谈到阿特拉斯时说：“我和那个家伙真的有问题。”“他是个聪明人，他说的东西我相信他没有任何真正的洞察力、知识或经验。他一直在说，当你把它解剖出来，解析出来的时候，这是没有任何意义的。“。
最值得注意的是，阿特拉斯普遍提倡群体免疫的危险想法，即让病毒不受控制地传播，同时试图保护弱势群体--世界卫生组织(World Health Organization)总干事最近称这种想法“在道德上是不合情理的，也是不可行的”。
福奇在谈到阿特拉斯时说：“他坚称自己不是在推动群体豁免权的人。”“他说，‘我不是这个意思。’(但)他说的每一句话--当你把它们放在一起并缝合在一起时--一切都是朝着这样的理念发展的：“即使人们感染了病毒，也不会有任何不同。”纯属浪费时间。口罩不起作用。谁在乎，‘你唯一需要做的就是保护脆弱的人，比如疗养院里的人，“福奇说。
福奇接着说：“如果你不死，一切都会好起来，这种虚假故事的想法根本不是这样的。”“但是要说‘让人们感染，没关系，只要确保人们不会死就行了’--对我来说，作为一个行医50年的人，这完全说不通。”
周六，就在福奇的采访刊登在“华盛顿邮报”的同一天，阿特拉斯接受了采访，在采访中，他继续为政府的处理方式和他对口罩和疾病传播的立场进行辩护。值得注意的是，采访是在俄罗斯国家支持的RT网络上进行的，该网络注册为外国代理，这意味着它正在制造宣传，试图影响美国的舆论、政策和法律。
周日，阿特拉斯为坐在那里接受采访道歉，他在推特上说，他“不知道他们是注册的外国特工”。
他说：“我很后悔接受采访，并为让自己被占了便宜而道歉。”“我特别向正在努力保卫我们的国家安全界道歉。”
周一，特朗普在竞选活动中继续淡化这种流行病。他最近在推特上说，“我们有更多的病例，因为我们有更多的检测！”这也是错误的；根据COVID跟踪项目的说法，检测的增加不足以解释病例的激增。
到目前为止，美国已有超过920万人感染了这种大流行病毒，超过23.1万人死亡。每天平均7天的新病例超过8万例，创历史新高。目前美国有超过4.7万人住院，平均每天死亡人数超过800人。
note_en:
Ten months into the pandemic and the outlook for the United States is worse than ever. Coronavirus cases are spiking to unseen levels across the country, with a record of nearly over 97,000 cases reported on Friday alone. As hospitals in dozens of states begin filling, health experts look wearily to the weeks ahead. Cold weather and a series of holidays will undoubtedly bring people indoors and together, showering gasoline on the current inferno of disease transmission.
 Yet, despite the grim reality, President Donald Trump and his administration have spent the last days before the presidential election not addressing the dire crisis, but downplaying the tragedy and attacking public health experts and frontline workers. Trump’s coronavirus advisor, Scott Atlas, even  appeared on a Russian propaganda television show to defend the administration’s response, in which he echoed his previous false and  misleading claims about the pandemic.
 On Friday, Trump himself  falsely claimed doctors are  inflating the number of coronavirus deaths for money. In response, the president of the American Medical Association, Susan Bailey, called Trump’s false statement “ malicious, outrageous, and completely misguided.”
 “Rather than attacking us and lobbing baseless charges at physicians, our leaders should be following the science and urging adherence to the public health steps we know work—wearing a mask, washing hands and practicing physical distancing,” Dr. Bailey said in a statement.
 Nevertheless, Trump’s senior advisor, Jason Miller,  doubled down on the false statement in a Sunday with ABC’s  This Week, baselessly suggesting that “There have been all sorts of independent things pointing to that.”
 At a late-night rally on Sunday, Trump went on to suggest he would fire the country’s top infectious disease expert, Anthony Fauci, after the election. The comment came after Trump made his oft-repeated complaints that the raging public health crisis in the United States is receiving more news coverage than it should. This prompted his audience to begin chanting “ Fire Fauci! Fire Fauci.”
 “Don’t tell anybody,” Trump responded, “but let me wait until a little bit after the election. I appreciate the advice.”
  Throughout his late-stage campaigning, Trump has held ( mostly maskless) rallies and touted the idea that his administration has successfully responded to the pandemic. He has repeatedly stated the  false claim that the United States is “rounding the turn” on the pandemic.
  “We’re in for a whole lot of hurt. It’s not a good situation,” Fauci  told The Washington Post in an interview late Friday. “All the stars are aligned in the wrong place as you go into the fall and winter season, with people congregating at home indoors. You could not possibly be positioned more poorly.”
 In the interview, Fauci also blasted Scott Atlas, who is Trump’s most trusted advisor on the coronavirus crisis despite not having a background in infectious disease.
 “I have real problems with that guy,” Fauci said of Atlas in his interview with the Post. “He’s a smart guy who’s talking about things that I believe he doesn’t have any real insight or knowledge or experience in. He keeps talking about things that when you dissect it out and parse it out, it doesn’t make any sense.”
 Most notably, Atlas has generally  promoted the dangerous idea of  herd immunity, or letting the virus spread uncontrolled while trying to shield the vulnerable—an idea the Director-General of the World Health Organization recently called “ morally unconscionable and unfeasible.”
 “He insists he’s not somebody who’s pushing for herd immunity,” Fauci said of Atlas. “He says, ‘That’s not what I mean.’ [But] everything he says—when you put them together and stitch them together—everything is geared toward the concept of ‘It doesn’t make any difference if people get infected. It’s a waste of time. Masks don’t work. Who cares,’ and the only thing you need to do is protect the vulnerable, like people in the nursing homes,” Fauci said.
 “The idea of this false narrative that if you don’t die, everything is hunky dory is just not the case,” Fauci went on. “But to say, ‘Let people get infected, it doesn’t matter, just make sure people don’t die’—to me, as a person who’s been practicing medicine for 50 years, it doesn’t make any sense at all.”
  On Saturday, the same day Fauci’s interview ran in the Post, Atlas sat for an interview in which he continued to defend the administration’s handling and his stance on masks and disease spread. Notably, the interview was with Russia’s state-backed RT network,  which is registered as foreign agent, meaning it is producing propaganda “attempting to influence US public opinion, policy, and laws."
 On Sunday, Atlas apologized for sitting for the interview, saying in  a tweet that he was “unaware they are a registered foreign agent.”
 “I regret doing the interview and apologize for allowing myself to be taken advantage of,” he said. “I especially apologize to the national security community who is working hard to defend us.”
 On Monday, Trump continued to downplay the pandemic on the campaign trail. He recently  tweeted that “We have more Cases because we have more Testing!” This is also false; increases in testing are not sufficient to explain the spike in cases,  according to The COVID Tracking Project.
 So far, more than 9.2 million people in the United States have been infected with the pandemic virus, and over 231,000 have died. The seven-day average of new daily cases is at a record high of over 80,000. More than 47,000 people in the US are currently hospitalized, and the seven-day average of deaths per day is over 800.
title: 特朗普顾问转向俄罗斯宣传电视兜售失败的大流行应对措施
title_en: Trump advisor turns to Russian propaganda TV to tout failed pandemic response

据报道，索尼采取法律行动后，一家销售定制PS5面板的外围公司被迫取消所有订单，并将产品撤出销售。
在索尼本周早些时候提出投诉后，PlateStation5.com已经被迫将品牌更名为CustomizeMyPlates.com，但现在卖家声称，随后因定制面板而诉诸法庭的威胁已迫使其完全停止销售该产品。
CustomizeMyPlates通过电子邮件告诉VGC：“在我们推出之前，我们做了尽职调查，并认为，因为索尼只在面板上有未决的专利，所以不会有问题。”
“但我们的网站上线仅一天后，索尼的律师就以商标侵权为由要求我们(当时的PlateStation5)改名。我们认为这一转变足以让每个人都满意，老实说，我们也希望如此，因为我们已经在进行我们的产品开发了。
“但后来索尼的律师告诉我们，这是他们的意见，索尼的知识产权延伸到了面板，如果我们继续在任何国家销售和分销这些产品，我们最终会被告上法庭。”
该公司补充说：“这一切都是昨天曝光的，我们现在将取消并退还全球范围内的所有面板订单…。我们对此极为失望，但我们别无选择。“。
该公司表示，今后它将在商标法和专利法方面变得“更明智”，并专注于提供合法的控制台皮肤和其他产品。
这家总部位于英国的公司正在接受各种颜色的非官方PS5外壳的订单，价格为39.99美元/GB 32，并表示希望在游戏机上市后两周内将其发货给客户。
该公司表示，它正在与英国和中国的制造商合作制造面板，据称这些面板是由基于PS5官方发布的尺寸的“优质行业标准塑料”制成的。
正如上个月一段官方的PlayStation5拆卸视频所显示的那样，PS5的标准白色外壳折断了，理论上可以很容易地将非官方的复制品插入它的位置。
note_en:
A peripheral company selling custom PS5 faceplates has been forced to cancel all its orders and pull the products from sale, following reported legal action from  Sony.
 PlateStation5.com had already been forced to rebrand to  CustomizeMyPlates.com following a complaint from Sony earlier this week, but now the seller claims that subsequent threats to go to court over the custom faceplates have forced it to stop selling the product entirely.
  “Before we launched, we did our due diligence and were of the opinion, that because Sony only had pending patents on the faceplates there would be no problem,” CustomizeMyPlates told VGC via e-mail.
 “But after only a day of our website being live, Sony’s lawyers asked us to change our name (at the time PlateStation5), due to trademark infringements. We thought this switch would be enough to keep everyone happy, and honestly were hoping so since we were already underway with our product development.
  “But then Sony’s lawyers told us it was their opinion, Sony’s intellectual property extended to the faceplates, and that if we continued to sell and distribute them in any country, we would end up in court.”
 It added: “This all came to light yesterday and we are now cancelling and refunding all faceplate orders worldwide… we are extremely disappointed about this but we have no other option.”
 The firm said that moving forward it would be “wiser” on trademark and patent laws and focus on offering legal console skins and other products.
 The UK-based company was taking orders for the unofficial PS5 shells in a variety of colours for $39.99 / £32 and said it hoped to ship them to customers within two weeks of the console’s launch date.
 The company said it was working with manufacturers in the UK and China to create the faceplates, which were claimed to be made from “premium industry standard plastic” based on the PS5’s officially released dimensions.
 As revealed in  an official PlayStation 5 teardown video last month, PS5’s standard white shell snaps off, theoretically allowing the unofficial replicas to be easily inserted in its place.
title: PS5面板销售商取消所有订单，此前索尼采取法律行动
title_en: PS5 Faceplate Seller Cancels All Orders Following 'Legal Action from Sony'

note:
SpaceX Starlink测试版用户开始分享他们的经验，证实了这项卫星服务可以在偏远地区提供快速的宽带速度和低的延迟。一位名为流浪编码器的Reddit测试者将他的新Starlink设备和便携式电源带到了爱达荷州的一个国家森林，在那里他以120 Mbps的下载速度连接到互联网。
他昨天写道，StarLink效果非常好。我做了一个实时视频通话和一些测试。我的电源是最大300W，激活时整个系统的耗电量约为116W。在这个地方，流浪编码器无法从依赖T-Mobile和美国蜂窝网络的Google Fi获得任何蜂窝服务。这里没有任何运营商的牢房，他写道。
流浪编码器使用森林中的Starlink连接在Reddit上发布了这篇帖子，并将一系列照片上传到Imgur。流浪编码器告诉Ars，他在北爱达荷州的家里使用Starlink，他是在爱达荷州狭长地带的海登溪射击场进行测试的，地点是爱达荷州的核心国家森林。
以下是流浪编码器在森林中测试的一些照片和用户终端在他家外面的照片：
将Starlink用户终端/卫星天线放置在森林中相对开阔的地面上，流浪编码器进行了速度测试，测试了下载速度为120 Mbps，上传速度为12 Mbps，延迟为37ms。他在一个不同的，森林更茂密的地方得到了更差的结果，在那里他把碟子放在离树更近的地方，因为Starlink需要清晰的视线才能看到SpaceX卫星。漫游编码器写道，与预期的一样，它在视线正对的大树树冠/树木的情况下效果不佳。我一次只能连接大约5秒钟。确保你尽可能清晰地看到天空！&#34；
漫游编码器没有敦促其他Starlink测试员完成同样的实验，以防SpaceX反对Beta测试员在远离注册地点使用这项服务。他写道，从所有方面考虑，最好将[用户终端]保持在注册的位置，直到这个话题有了官方的说明。不过，仅仅知道移动是可能的，这是件好事。
流浪编码器也在另一篇Reddit帖子中分享了初步印象，并在他家分享了一系列设备的照片。以下是拆卸设备的一些照片：
所有的东西都有极高的构建质量，这比我想象的要好得多，流浪编码器写道。感觉像是来自未来。考虑到一部顶级手机的价格在1000美元左右，我完全惊讶于我能花500美元买到这样的设备，所以我对这项服务有积极的偏见。
正如上周透露的那样，Starlink测试版的费用是每月99美元，外加499美元的用户终端、安装三脚架和路由器的预付费用。
在家里，漫游编码器说，当盘子放在地面上，盘子与天空之间的障碍物有限时，他的下载速度为135 Mbps，上传速度为25 Mbps，延迟为21ms。他写道，他还在另一个地方用严重的障碍物测试了用户终端，比如恶劣的天气、树梢、栅栏和房屋。即使在这种情况下，他报告的下载速度为46 Mbps，上传速度为15 Mbps，延迟为41ms。当他进行测试时，他还没有把天线放在屋顶上。
他写道，考虑到目前这种联系面临的所有障碍，我对它的工作效果感到惊讶。这项服务完全可以访问流媒体、低延迟视频会议和游戏。即使是测试版，他们似乎也低估了Starlink的能力，所以我很兴奋地看到它成熟了。
虽然在屋顶上安装一个用户终端肯定会很复杂，但漫游编码器写道，其他的一切都很简单：
接通电源后，启动大约需要1分钟，然后需要1分钟到15分钟的时间才能机械地正确定位。定位都是自动完成的，所以基本上就是即插即用。从天线阵列到电源有一条单电源以太网线，从电源到路由器有一条单电源以太网线。提供的天线线是固定在天线上的，但远远超过100&#39；(据估计，我没有测量长度)。除了物理放置和设置Wi-Fi网络的名称和密码之外，不涉及任何设置-所有棘手的事情都是自动完成的。
由Ookla收集并由PCMag上周发布的新速度测试数据发现，10月份Starlink的平均下载速度为79.5 Mbps，平均上传速度为13.8 Mbps，当时该服务处于更有限的测试期。同样的数据发现，Viasat的Exude服务和HughesNet的平均下载速度分别为24.75 Mbps和19.84 Mbps，这两种服务都提供地球同步卫星服务。Viasat和HughesNet的上传速度分别为3.25 Mbps和2.64 Mbps。
StarLink的低地球轨道卫星在延迟时间上大大超过了高轨道卫星，平均为42ms。根据PCMag的数据，ViaSat和HughesNet分别为643ms和728ms。
SpaceX上周向新用户发出的邀请函告诉他们，在接下来的几个月里，随着我们增强Starlink系统，预计数据速度在50 Mbps到150 Mbps之间，延迟在20ms到40ms之间。至于延迟，我们预计到2021年夏天将达到16ms到19ms。
一位蒙大拿州居民发布了速度测试结果，下载速度为174 Mbps，上传速度为33 Mbps，延迟为39ms。在蒙大拿州的乡村，我们的另一种选择是按演出付费。这位测试者昨天在Reddit上写道，StarLink将永远改变这个游戏。
Starlink Reddit社区有几个用于跟踪测试版进度的资源，例如用户速度测试列表和州列表，其中至少有一些人收到了测试版邀请。这些州是爱达荷州、密歇根州、明尼苏达州、蒙大拿州、俄勒冈州、华盛顿州和威斯康星州。你也可以查看一位用户的10秒视频，以加速了解拆箱过程。
note_en:
SpaceX Starlink beta users are starting to share their experiences, confirming that the satellite service can provide fast broadband speeds and low latencies in remote areas. A beta tester who goes by the Reddit username Wandering-coder brought his new Starlink equipment and a portable power supply to a national forest in Idaho, where he connected to the Internet with 120Mbps download speeds.
 Starlink "works beautifully," he  wrote yesterday. "I did a real-time video call and some tests. My power supply is max 300w, and the drain for the whole system while active was around 116w." Starlink pulled that off in a place where Wandering-coder couldn't get any cellular service from Google Fi, which relies on the T-Mobile and US Cellular networks. "There is no cell here with any carrier," he wrote.
 Wandering-coder used Starlink connectivity in the forest to make that post on Reddit and to upload a  series of pictures to Imgur. Wandering-coder told Ars that he uses Starlink at home in North Idaho and that he conducted his test at the Hayden Creek Shooting Range in the Idaho panhandle's  Coeur d'Alene National Forest.
 Here are some pictures from Wandering-coder's test in the forest and shots of the user terminal outside his home:
    With the Starlink user terminal/satellite dish placed on the ground in a relatively open part of the forest, Wandering-coder did a speed test that measured downloads of 120Mbps, uploads of 12Mbps, and latency of 37ms. He got worse results in a different, more heavily forested location where he placed the dish  closer to the trees, because Starlink needs a clear line of sight to SpaceX satellites. "It didn't work well with a heavy tree canopy/trees directly in the line of sight, as expected," Wandering-coder wrote. "I would be connected only for about 5 seconds at a time. Make sure you have as clear a view of the sky as possible!"
 Wandering-coder is not urging other Starlink testers to complete the same experiment, in case SpaceX objects to beta testers using the service away from their registered location. "All things considered, [it's] probably best to keep [the user terminal] where registered until there is official illumination on the topic," he  wrote. "Just knowing mobility is possible, though, is nice."
  Wandering-coder also shared initial impressions in  another Reddit post and a  series of pictures of the equipment at his home. Here are some shots from unboxing the equipment:
    "Everything is of an extreme build quality, and this works significantly better than I had ever imagined," Wandering-coder wrote. "It feels like it's from the future. Given a top-tier cell phone costs in the $1,000 range, I am completely amazed I have my hands on a setup like this for ~$500, so I am biased positively towards this service."
 As  revealed last week, the Starlink beta costs $99 a month plus $499 upfront for the user terminal, mounting tripod, and router.
 At home, Wandering-coder says he got 135Mbps download speeds, 25Mbps uploads, and 21ms latency when the dish was placed in a ground-level spot with "limited obstruction" between the dish and sky. He also tested the user terminal in a different spot with "significant obstruction" in the form of "bad weather, treetops, fences, [and] houses," he wrote. Even in that scenario, he reported download speeds of 46Mbps, upload speeds of 15Mbps, and 41ms latency. He hadn't placed the antenna on his roof yet when he conducted the tests.
 "Given all the obstructions for this connection at the moment, I am amazed at how well it works," he wrote. "Streaming, low-latency video conferencing, and gaming are all completely accessible with this service. Even for the beta, it appears as though they've under-estimated Starlink's capabilities, so I am excited to see it mature."
 Though mounting a user terminal on one's roof is bound to be complicated, Wandering-coder wrote that everything else is pretty simple:
 Bootup is roughly 1 minute when plugged into power, then it takes anywhere from 1 minute to 15 minutes to properly orient mechanically. Positioning is all done automatically, so it's basically plug & play... There is a single powered Ethernet wire from the antenna array to the power supply, and a single powered Ethernet wire from the power supply to the router. The provided antenna wire is fixed to the antenna, but well over 100' (by estimation, I didn't measure the length). There is no setup involved besides physical placement and setting a name of the Wi-Fi network and password—all the tricky stuff is automated.
  New speed-test data collected by Ookla and  published by PCMag last week found average Starlink download speeds of 79.5Mbps and average upload speeds of 13.8Mbps in October, when the service was in a more limited beta. The same data found average download speeds of 24.75Mbps for Viasat's Exede service and 19.84Mbps for HughesNet, both of which offer service from geostationary satellites. Upload speeds for Viasat and HughesNet were 3.25Mbps and 2.64Mbps, respectively.
 Starlink's low Earth orbit satellites greatly outperformed the higher-orbit satellites on latency, with Starlink posting a 42ms average. Viasat and HughesNet came in at 643ms and 728ms, respectively, according to PCMag.
 SpaceX's invitations to new users last week told them to expect "data speeds vary[ing] from 50Mbps to 150Mbps and latency from 20ms to 40ms over the next several months as we enhance the Starlink system... For latency, we expect to achieve 16ms to 19ms by summer 2021."
 One Montana resident posted a  speed test result with a 174Mbps download speed, 33Mbps upload speed, and 39ms latency. "Way out in rural Montana where our alternative is to pay by the gig. Starlink will forever change the game," the beta tester wrote on Reddit yesterday.
 The Starlink Reddit community has several  resources for tracking beta progress, such as a  list of user speed tests and a  list of states where at least some people received beta invitations. Those states are Idaho, Michigan, Minnesota, Montana, Oregon, Washington, and Wisconsin. You can also check out  one user's 10-second video for a sped-up look at the unboxing process.
title: SpaceX Starlink测试仪将用户终端带入森林，获得120 Mbps
title_en: SpaceX Starlink beta tester takes user terminal into forest, gets 120Mbps

好消息是，2020年不是2016年。社交网络做好了更好的准备，以应对广泛的复杂、危险或其他模棱两可的选举日情景。
坏消息是：2020年是它自己的野兽，它给这个分裂的国家带来了噩梦般的健康场景，与四年前相比，这个国家现在更容易受到错误信息、过度党派之争和危险想法从边缘转向中心的影响。
外国对2016年大选的干预让美国措手不及，但要让一个在过去八个月里一直预计最坏情况会趋同的国家并不是那么容易的。
社交平台以一种2016年没有的方式为2020年大选做好了准备。以下是他们所担心的，以及他们将从过去四年中吸取的重要教训。
特朗普总统多次表示，如果他输了，他不会接受选举结果-这是一个可能危及美国民主的令人震惊的威胁，但一个社交平台一直在密切跟踪。近几个月来，特朗普在社交网络上反复无常、经常违反规则的行为充当了一种压力测试，让这些平台可以为选举设定不同的情景。
特别是Facebook和Twitter已经制定了详细的计划，如果选举结果不立即明朗，或者如果候选人在计票后拒绝接受官方结果，会发生什么。
在选举之夜，Facebook将在Facebook和Instagram的顶部别上一条消息，告诉用户计票工作仍在进行中。当权威结果出现时，Facebook将更改这些消息，以反映官方结果。重要的是，美国大选结果可能在选举之夜或之后的几天内不会明朗，这是Facebook和其他社交网络正在准备迎接的潜在结果。
如果候选人过早宣布获胜，Facebook不会表示会删除这些声明，但它会将这些声明与其信息配对，即没有官方结果，投票仍在进行中。
推特在两个月前发布了处理选举结果的计划，解释说在权威的选举结果出来之前，它将移除或给过早宣布胜利的人贴上警告标签。该公司还明确表示，将对任何“煽动非法行为以阻止权力和平移交或有序继承”的推文采取行动，这是一个必须阐明的令人震惊的规则，但在2020年是必要的。
周一，Twitter详细阐述了自己的政策，称将专注于给有关总统选举和其他有争议的竞选的推文贴上误导性标签。该公司发布了一张标签样本图片，显示一条警告，称“这条推文分享的信息不准确。”
我们可能会将从选举之夜开始的推文贴上标签，这些推文在正式宣布选举结果之前就对选举结果做出了声明。
我们将优先考虑总统选举和其他竞争激烈的竞选，在这些竞选中，可能存在误导性信息的重大问题。Pic.twitter.com/BExhZdVMnB。
上周，该公司还开始在订阅源的顶部向用户显示大量错误信息警告。这些信息告诉用户，他们“可能会遇到误导性的信息”关于邮寄投票，并警告他们，选举结果可能不会立即知道。
据推特称，试图分享带有误导性选举相关错误信息的推文的用户将看到一个弹出窗口，指示他们指向经过审查的信息，并迫使他们在分享之前点击警告。推特还表示，它将对任何可能对投票产生怀疑的“有争议的指控”采取行动，包括“关于选举舞弊、篡改选票、计票或选举结果认证的未经核实的信息”。
许多用户可能已经注意到的另一个重大变化是Twitter决定禁止转发。用户仍然可以通过点击弹出页面来转发，但Twitter做出了改变，鼓励人们引用转发。减缓错误信息传播的努力令人震惊，Twitter表示，这一努力将至少持续到选举周结束。
YouTube没有详细说明其决策过程，但该公司此前表示，将在与选举相关的搜索结果和与选举相关的视频下方贴上“信息性”标签。标签警告用户“结果可能不是最终结果”，并将他们指向该公司的选举信息中心。
这是社交网络取得长足进步的一个领域。四年前，俄罗斯的虚假信息在社交平台上扎根后，这些公司现在相互协调，并与政府就他们看到的威胁进行协调。
在2016年之后，Facebook最终意识到，其平台可以被用来扩大仇恨和错误信息等社会弊病的规模。它的记分卡参差不齐，但它针对外国虚假信息的行动一直很有力，大大减少了这种威胁。
2016年同样的担忧不太可能重演。Facebook做出了积极的努力，在其平台上寻找外国协调的虚假信息活动，并定期发布发现的内容，几乎没有延迟。但在2020年，最大的担忧来自国内-而不是没有。
到目前为止，大多数外国信息业务规模都很小，未能获得太多牵引力。上个月，Facebook删除了一个与伊朗有关的虚假账户网络。这次行动规模很小，未能产生太多牵引力，但它表明美国对手仍有兴趣尝试这一战术。
为了解决人们对广告中选举错误信息的担忧，Facebook选择了暂时的政治广告停电，从11月4日太平洋时间上午12点开始，一直持续到该公司认为可以安全地重新打开广告为止。自10月27日以来，Facebook没有接受任何新的政治广告，此前曾表示，不会接受任何使选举结果合法化的广告。谷歌还将在周二投票结束后暂停与选举相关的广告。
自2016年以来，Facebook对政治广告进行了多项重大改变，当时俄罗斯购买了Facebook广告，以干预美国政治。该平台上的政治广告现在受到更严格的审查和更高的透明度，Facebook的广告库成为一个模范工具，让任何人都可以看到发布了什么广告，谁购买了这些广告，以及他们花了多少钱。
与Facebook不同，Twitter处理政治广告的方式是完全切断它。该公司在一年前宣布了这一变化，此后再也没有回头。TikTok还选择不允许政治广告。
我们已经决定在全球范围内停止推特上的所有政治广告。我们认为，政治信息的影响应该是赢得的，而不是收买的。为什么？…的几个原因。🧵
本周，出于政治动机的暴力是美国的一大担忧-这一担忧表明，在特朗普执政的四年时间里，局势变得多么紧张。在周二之前，总统多次谎称选民欺诈，并鼓励他的追随者参与选民恐吓，Facebook对这种威胁有足够的线索，以至于它制定了一项政策，禁止围绕投票观察的“军事化”语言。
Facebook最近做出了其他一些有意义的变化，比如禁止危险的亲特朗普阴谋论QAnon和利用该平台组织的民兵组织，尽管这些努力在游戏中来得很晚。
Facebook因在今年早些时候种族正义抗议期间对特朗普的一篇帖子不作为而受到广泛批评，该帖子警告称，“当抢劫开始时，枪击就开始了”，但它最近的姿态表明，现在可能会更认真地对待类似的帖子。本周我们将关注Facebook如何应对新出现的暴力威胁。
它最近针对极端主义的果断举措很重要，但该平台长期以来一直在孵化利用该公司的网络和活动工具聚集在一起的团体，以应对潜在的现实世界暴力。即使他们不再被允许在该平台上使用，这些小组中的许多人都被组织起来，然后将他们的网络转移到替代的社交网络和私人渠道上。尽管如此，让在主流社交网络上组织暴力变得更加困难是朝着正确方向迈出的一大步。
推特还提前解决了与选举相关的暴力的潜在威胁，指出可能会增加警告，或要求用户删除任何“煽动干预选举”或鼓励暴力的推文。
Facebook是美国政治生活上演的最大在线舞台。虽然有类似数量的美国人在YouTube上观看视频，但Facebook是他们与候选人较量的地方，分享新闻故事(一些是合法的，一些是不合法的)，并通常在政治上表达自己。正如我们所经历的艰辛教训，这让它成为了一个火药桶，特别是在选举期间。
虽然Facebook在2016年之后迅速对外国威胁采取了行动，但该公司在可能被视为出于政治动机的平台改革上拖拖拉拉-这种犹豫不决产生了适得其反的效果，孵化了危险的极端分子，并允许各种错误信息--尤其是极右翼分子--得以生存和蓬勃发展。
但在过去的几个月里，无论是受到竞争选举、联邦反垄断行动或拜登可能当选总统的威胁的启发，Facebook都通过一系列积极的政策变化发出了转向更激进的温和政策的信号。随之而来的一系列以选举为重点的播客和电视广告表明，Facebook也担心公众的看法-这是一件好事。
推特的选举计划沟通良好，内容详尽。2020年，该公司将以更高的透明度对待其政策决策，实时沟通，并不怕承认错误。这个相对较小的社交网络在发布政治内容方面扮演着超大的角色，而这些内容在其他地方被放大了，所以它所做的选择对于打击错误信息和极端主义至关重要。
自2016年以来，主持和放大在线政治对话的公司已经吸取了一些重要教训-大多是艰难的。让我们只希望这足以帮助他们引导他们的摇摇欲坠的平台度过美国现代史上最令人担忧的时刻之一。
note_en:
The good news? 2020 isn’t 2016. Social networks are way better prepared to handle a wide array of complex, dangerous or otherwise ambiguous Election Day scenarios.
 The bad news: 2020 is its own beast, one that’s unleashed a nightmare health scenario on a divided nation that’s even more susceptible now to misinformation, hyper-partisanship and dangerous ideas moving from the fringe to the center than it was four years ago.
  The U.S. was caught off guard by foreign interference in the 2016 election, but shocking a nation that’s spent the last eight months expecting a convergence of worst-case scenarios won’t be so easy.
 Social platforms have braced for the 2020 election in a way they didn’t in 2016. Here’s what they’re worried about and the critical lessons from the last four years that they’ll bring to bear.
  President Trump has repeatedly signaled that he won’t accept the results of the election in the case that he loses — a shocking threat that could imperil American democracy, but one social platforms have been tracking closely. Trump’s erratic, often rule-bending behavior on social networks in recent months has served as a kind of stress test, allowing those platforms to game out different scenarios for the election.
 Facebook   and Twitter in particular have laid out detailed plans about what happens if the results of the election aren’t immediately clear or if a candidate refuses to accept official results once they’re tallied.
 On election night,  Facebook will pin a message to the top of both Facebook and Instagram telling users that vote counting is still underway. When authoritative results are in, Facebook will change those messages to reflect the official results. Importantly, U.S. election results might not be clear on election night or for some days afterward, a potential outcome that Facebook and other social networks are bracing for.
  If a candidate declared victory prematurely, Facebook doesn’t say it will remove those claims, but it will pair them with its message that there’s no official result and voting is still underway.
 Twitter    released its plans for handling election results two months ago, explaining that it will either remove or attach a warning label to premature claims of victory before authoritative election results are in. The company also explicitly stated that it will act against any tweets “inciting unlawful conduct to prevent a peaceful transfer of power or orderly succession,” a shocking rule to have to articulate, but a necessary one in 2020.
 On Monday, Twitter  elaborated on its policy, saying that it would focus on labeling misleading tweets about the presidential election and other contested races. The company released a sample image of a label it would append, showing a warning stating that “this tweet is sharing inaccurate information.”
 We may label Tweets, starting on election night, that make claims about election results before they’re officially called.
 We’ll be prioritizing the presidential election and other highly contested races where there may be significant issues with misleading information.  pic.twitter.com/BExhZdVMnB
  Last week, the company also began  showing users large misinformation warnings at the top of their feeds. The messages told users that they “might encounter misleading information” about mail-in voting and also cautioned them that election results may not be immediately known.
 According to Twitter, users who try to share tweets with misleading election-related misinformation will see a pop-up pointing them to vetted information and forcing them to click through a warning before sharing. Twitter also says it will act on any “disputed claims” that might cast doubt on voting, including “unverified information about election rigging, ballot tampering, vote tallying, or certification of election results.”
 One other major change that many users probably already noticed is  Twitter’s decision to disable retweets. Users can still retweet by clicking through a pop-up page, but Twitter made the change to encourage people to quote retweet instead. The effort to slow down the spread of misinformation was striking, and Twitter said it will stay in place through the end of election week, at least.
  YouTube didn’t go into similar detail about its decision making, but the  company previously said it will put an “informational” label on search results related to the election and below election-related videos. The label warns users that “results may not be final” and points them to the company’s election info hub.
  This is one area where social networks have made big strides. After Russian disinformation  took root on social platforms four years ago, those companies now coordinate with one another and the government about the threats they’re seeing.
 In the aftermath of 2016, Facebook eventually woke up to the idea that its platform could be leveraged to scale social ills like hate and misinformation. Its scorecard is uneven, but its actions against foreign disinformation have been  robust, reducing that threat considerably.
 A repeat of the same concerns from 2016 is unlikely. Facebook made aggressive efforts to find foreign coordinated disinformation campaigns across its platforms, and it publishes what it finds regularly and with little delay. But in 2020, the biggest concerns are coming from within the country — not without.
 Most foreign information operations have been small so far, failing to gain much traction. Last month, Facebook removed  a network of fake accounts connected to Iran. The operation was small and failed to generate much traction, but it shows that U.S. adversaries are still interested in trying out the tactic.
  To address concerns around election misinformation in ads, Facebook opted for a temporary political ad blackout, starting at 12AM PT on November 4 and continuing until the company deems it safe to toggle them back on. Facebook hasn’t accepted any new political ads  since October 27 and previously said it won’t accept any ads that  delegitimize the results of the election. Google will also pause election-related ads after polls close Tuesday.
 Facebook has made a number of big changes to political ads since 2016, when  Russia bought Facebook ads to meddle with U.S. politics. Political ads on the platform are subject to more scrutiny and much more transparency now and  Facebook’s ad library emerged as an exemplary tool that allows anyone to see what ads have been published, who bought them and how much they spent.
 Unlike Facebook, Twitter’s way of dealing with political advertising was  cutting it off entirely. The company announced the change a year ago and hasn’t looked back since. TikTok also opted to  disallow political ads.
 We’ve made the decision to stop all political advertising on Twitter globally. We believe political message reach should be earned, not bought. Why? A few reasons…🧵
  Politically-motivated violence is a big worry this week in the U.S. — a concern that shows just how tense the situation has grown under four years of Trump. Leading into Tuesday, the president has repeatedly made  false claims of voter fraud and encouraged his followers to engage in voter intimidation, a threat Facebook was clued into enough that it made a policy  prohibiting “militarized” language around poll watching.
  Facebook made a number of other meaningful recent changes like  banning the dangerous pro-Trump conspiracy theory QAnon and  militias that use the platform to organize, though those efforts have come very late in the game.
 Facebook was  widely criticized for its inaction around a Trump post warning “when the looting starts, the shooting starts” during racial justice protests earlier this year, but its recent posture suggests similar posts might be taken more seriously now. We’ll be watching how Facebook handles emerging threats of violence this week.
 Its recent decisive moves against extremism are important, but the platform has long incubated groups that use the company’s networking and event tools to come together for potential real-world violence. Even if they aren’t allowed on the platform any longer, many of those groups got organized and then moved their networks onto alternative social networks and private channels. Still, making it more difficult to organize violence on mainstream social networks is a big step in the right direction.
 Twitter also addressed the potential threat of election-related violence in advance, noting that it may add warnings or require users to remove any tweets “inciting interference with the election” or encouraging violence.
  Facebook is the biggest online arena where U.S. political life plays out. While a similar number of Americans watch videos on YouTube, Facebook is where they go to duke it over candidates, share news stories (some legitimate, some not) and generally express themselves politically. And as we’ve learned the hard way, that makes it a tinderbox, especially during elections.
  While Facebook acted against foreign threats quickly after 2016, the company dragged its feet on platform changes that could be perceived as politically motivated — a hesitation that backfired by incubating dangerous extremists and allowing many kinds of misinformation, particularly on the far-right, to survive and thrive.
 But in the last few months, whether it was inspired by the threat of a contested election, federal antitrust action or a possible Biden presidency, Facebook has signaled a shift to more aggressive moderation with a flurry of positive policy changes. An accompanying flurry of election-focused podcast and television ads suggests Facebook is worried about public perception too — and that’s a good thing.
 Twitter’s plan for the election has been well-communicated and detailed. In 2020, the company treats its policy decisions with more transparency, communicates them in real-time and isn’t afraid to admit to mistakes. The relatively small social network plays an outsized role in publishing political content that’s amplified elsewhere so the choices it makes are critical for countering misinformation and extremism.
 The companies that host and amplify online political conversation have learned some major lessons since 2016 — mostly the hard way. Let’s just hope it was enough to help them guide their roiling platforms through one of the most fraught moments in modern U.S. history.
title: 自2016年大选以来，社交网络学到了什么
title_en: What social networks have learned since the 2016 election

note:
没有指定交易条款。但TikTok在声明中指出，扩大后的协议将使TikTok的创作者社区能够获得索尼音乐当前热门、新发行、新兴热门歌曲、标志性经典和深度剪辑目录中的声音片段。
该公司还表示，将与索尼合作，以支持“更高水平的TikTok用户个性化和创造力”，并“为粉丝与中小企业艺术家和音乐的互动提供新的、前瞻性的机会”，但没有透露细节。
这可能表明，两家公司可能会在推广努力方面展开合作，而不仅仅是展示索尼的音乐剪辑-也许，类似于标签营销或品牌效应之类的东西，将使人们能够更好地发现音乐或建立更好的粉丝联系。
报道称，TikTok今年早些时候已经与环球(Universal)、索尼(Sony)和华纳(Warner)达成了短期许可协议。这让唱片公司有更多的时间敲定与TikTok协议的细节，而不必在此期间将他们的音乐从平台上撤下。
根据“公告牌”的一份报告，TikTok现在将向索尼支付比之前的转播权协议“显著增加”的费用。TikTok目前尚未宣布与其他主要公司达成类似的扩大交易。
虽然TikTok最终不得不增加对唱片公司的支付，但它并不是没有自己的谈判力。这款视频应用程序已经证明了它有能力推动排行榜的上升，甚至为新艺人创造职业生涯。
尼尔森(Nielsen)去年表示，没有其他新兴应用程序比TikTok帮助打破了更多的歌曲。然后它指出，今年收听次数最多的点播歌曲，来自Lil Nas X的“Old City Road”，以及Ava Max的“Sweet But Spro”和Joji的“Slow Dance in the Dark”，都是TikTok营销能力的例子。
Billboard今天还指出，TikTok帮助推动了Doja Cat(“说吧”)和24kGoldn(“情绪”)等索尼艺人的热门歌曲，并帮助索尼发现了新的人才。报道称，例如，哥伦比亚签下了病毒式的TikTok艺人，包括Lil Nas X，Powfu，StaySolidRocky，Jawsh 685，Arizona Zervas和24kGoldn。
与此同时，索尼的新协议将包括吸血鬼周末、哈里·斯泰尔斯、迈克尔·杰克逊等艺人。
与TikTok的力量相关，Spotify今天刚刚为艺人推出了一个新的推广营销工具，让他们能够更好地利用TikTok驱动的趋势。此前不久，TikTok的一段病毒式视频令人难以置信地将弗利特伍德·麦克(Fleetwood Mac)的经典热门歌曲“梦想”(Dreams)送上了排行榜，在Spotify和Apple Music上都位居榜首。
除了它的热门制作能力，TikTok今天还暗示了它的力量，当艺术家们举办面对面演唱会的能力有限的时候，它可以帮助他们接触到流行的歌迷。
索尼音乐娱乐公司全球数字业务和美国销售部总裁丹尼斯·库克说：“短片已经发展成为音乐生态系统中令人兴奋的新部分，为音乐的整体增长和粉丝体验的方式做出了贡献。他补充说：“TikTok是这一领域的领先者，我们很高兴能与他们合作，推动音乐发现，扩大创意机会，并支持艺术家的职业生涯。”
TikTok近几个月来一直很活跃，在与索尼的交易之前，今年还在其平台上添加了普林斯(Prince)、乔治·迈克尔(George Michael)、约翰·列侬(John Lennon)等人的音乐。
note_en:
Deal terms were not specified. But the expanded agreement will give TikTok’s creator community access to sound clips from Sony  Music’s   catalog of current hits, new releases, emerging favorites, iconic classics and deep cuts, TikTok noted in its announcement.
 Without going into details, the company also said it would work with Sony to support “greater levels of TikTok user personalization and creativity” and “drive new and forward-looking opportunities for fan engagement with SME’s artists and music.”
  This could indicate the companies may together work on promotional efforts that extend beyond just featuring Sony’s music clips — perhaps, something like hashtag campaigns or branded effects that will enable better music discovery or fan connections.
 TikTok  had already struck short-term licensing deals with Universal, Sony and Warner earlier this year, reports indicated. This had allowed the labels more time to hammer out the particulars of their agreements with TikTok without having to yank their music from the platform in the interim.
 According to  a Billboard report, TikTok will now pay Sony a “notable increase” over its previous rights deal. TikTok has not yet announced similar expanded deals with other majors at this time.
 Though TikTok ultimately had to increase its payments to labels, it’s not without negotiating power of its own. The video app brings to its side of table a proven its ability to drive tracks up the charts and even make careers for newer artists.
 Nielsen  last year said that no other emerging app had helped break more songs than TikTok. It then pointed to the year’s most listened to on-demand song, “Old Town Road” from Lil Nas X, as well as Ava Max’s “Sweet But Psycho” and Joji’s “Slow Dancing in the Dark,” as examples of TikTok’s marketing power.
 Billboard today also noted that TikTok helped drive hits from Sony artists like Doja Cat (“Say So”) and 24kGoldn (“Mood”), and helped Sony discover new talent. Columbia, for example, signed viral TikTok artists including Lil Nas X, Powfu, StaySolidRocky, Jawsh 685, Arizona Zervas and 24kGoldn, the report said.
 The new Sony deal, meanwhile, will wrap in artists like Vampire Weekend, Harry Styles, Michael Jackson and others.
 Related to TikTok’s power,  Spotify just today launched a new promotional marketing tool for artists that allows them to better capitalize on TikTok-driven trends. Its release came about shortly after  a TikTok viral video unbelievably sent Fleetwood Mac’s classic hit “Dreams” back up the charts to hit No. 1 on both Spotify and Apple Music.
 In addition to its hit-making ability, TikTok today alluded to its power in helping artists reach fans amid a pandemic when their ability to host in-person concerts is limited.
  “Short form video clips have developed into an exciting new part of the music ecosystem that contribute to the overall growth of music and the way fans experience it,” said Dennis Kooker, President, Global Digital Business and U.S. Sales for Sony Music Entertainment. “TikTok is a leader in this space and we are pleased to be partnering with them to drive music discovery, expand opportunities for creativity and support artist careers,” he added.
 TikTok has been on roll in recent months, having also added music from  Prince,  George Michael,  John Lennon, and others to its platform this year ahead of the Sony deal.
title: TikTok与索尼音乐达成新的许可协议
title_en: TikTok strikes new licensing agreement with Sony Music

note:
周一，FCC无线竞争委员会宣布，新分配的Uniendo a波多黎各基金第二阶段融资部分将确保波多黎各各地的每个地点都能接入下载速度至少为100 Mbps的宽带互联网，三分之一的领土获得1Gbps的互联网。
这一里程碑将来自FCC将在10年内向两家公司提供1.271亿美元的资金：Liberty Communications和Claro波多黎各。在这1.271亿美元中，71.54美元将捐给Liberty Communications，该公司将负责连接波多黎各78个市政城市中的43个-相当于大陆上的县。剩下的556万美元将帮助Claro在其他35个城市建立宽带连接。总而言之，全港约120万个地方将通过这笔资金获得某种形式的高速宽带接入。
在6月份宣布的波多黎各基金Uniendo的前一阶段，FCC在2022年之前拨款2.379亿美元，帮助AT&amp；T-Mobile和Claro在波多黎各和美属维尔京群岛建设LTE和5G网络。
这一声明不太可能让每个人都满意。2017年飓风玛丽亚袭击波多黎各三年后，该地区仍在从自然灾害中恢复，冠状病毒大流行突显了整个地区对宽带互联网接入的需求是多么迫切。波多黎各人需要立即的帮助，而不是几年后。
note_en:
On Monday, the FCC’s Wireless Competition Board  announced that newly allocated financing part of Stage Two of the  Uniendo a Puerto Rico fund will ensure that every location across Puerto Rico will have access to broadband internet with download speeds of at least 100 Mbps, with one-third of the territory getting 1 Gbps internet.
 The milestone will come from $127.1 million in funding the FCC will provide over 10 years to two firms: Liberty Communications and Claro Puerto Rico. Of that $127.1 million, $71.54 will go to Liberty Communications, which will take care of connecting 43 of Puerto Rico’s 78 municipios — the equivalent to counties on the mainland. The remaining $55.56 million will help Claro build out broadband connections in the other 35 municipios. All told, the approximately 1.2 million places across the territory will get some form of high-speed broadband access through the funding.
  In a previous stage of Uniendo a Puerto Rico fund announced in  June, the FCC allocated $237.9 million through to 2022 to help AT&T, T-Mobile and Claro build out LTE and 5G networks across Puerto Rico and the US Virgin Islands.
 The announcement is unlikely to satisfy everyone. Three years after Hurricane Maria devastated Puerto Rico in 2017, the territory is still recovering from the natural disaster, and the coronavirus pandemic has highlighted just how  desperate the need for broadband internet access is across the territory. Puerto Ricans need immediate help, not a couple of years from now.
title: FCC资金旨在保证整个波多黎各的100 Mbps互联网
title_en: FCC Funding Aims To Guarantee 100 Mbps Internet Throughout Puerto Rico

note:
2017年3月20日，周一，拉斯维加斯大道上的车流。(Jeff Scheid/内华达州独立报)。
上一次我在我们州生日后立即写了一篇专栏文章，我的态度相对轻松--有何不可？天气很好。我和未婚夫去卡森市跑步。我的朋友和他的新婚妻子带着他的狗参加了内华达州日游行。我和我们现在的州长都抚摸着他的狗，尽管不是在同一时间。瑞安·邦迪(Ryan Bundy)本人也在竞选州长，他乘坐一辆自制的装甲运兵车经过。
一言以蔽之，一切都很正常。是的，当时内华达州显然有“正常”的空间，但一直都有，一直都有，而且永远都会有。
身为内华达州人总是意味着相信某种悖论。在文化上，我们喜欢为自己的独立和自力更生而自豪。事实上，我们最大的两个大都市区和我们的州首府都是从发源于外州的分水岭取水；我们生产的粮食比任何一个不属于新英格兰的州都少；我们的制造业产出比爱达荷州少(不是人均总产量)，更不用说我们的其他邻国了。
这不一定是件坏事。由于内华达州长期干旱的地形，我们州生产的粮食比应有的要多，如果法律允许的话，我们会生产更少的食物。制造业也会变得口渴，正如最近为我们州两个最大的工业园确保供水所做的努力所揭示的那样。因此，考虑到我们的资源限制，内华达州人会选择做好一件事-通常是其他州在其境内更不愿意支持的事情-而不是试图拥抱推动大多数其他州经济的例行勤奋，这是有道理的。
我们在自然资源方面的匮乏，历史上我们一直试图用无耻和专一来弥补。
不幸的是，在我们州156年的时间里，内华达州人被迫一次又一次地艰难地学习，做一件别人不想做的事情，并把它做好，一切都很好，直到两件事中的一件发生了-要么是人们决定他们想要我们比过去更专业的东西，要么是受到我们的榜样的启发，他们认为我们提供的东西对他们的后院来说毕竟不是那么坏。
第一次发生在一个多世纪前的采矿业。在19世纪，Comstock Lode-它保持了近20年的重型和稳定的生产-为整个内华达州的其他采矿作业设定了非常不切实际的期望。地下有足够的矿石，可以产生几个百万富翁，并建造一个拥有所有现代便利设施的小镇，这些设施都是从无到有，从无到有。考虑到这个唯一的例子，拥有加州黄金和康斯托克(Comstock)白银的旧金山金融家非常乐意投入资金建设城镇，以换取成为下一次黄金或白银热潮的第一个地点。
可惜没有--至少没有康斯托克的规模。
像托诺帕、戈德菲尔德和流利特这样的小镇被证明是20世纪初的网络泡沫。他们每个人一开始都怀有世界一流的雄心，并相应地建立了物质上的舒适，结果发现他们每个人最多都坐在几年的矿石上。即使是最初掀起城市建设潮流的弗吉尼亚州，也已经开始衰落。就像乒乓球桌和办公室餐饮在20世纪90年代末成为硅谷过度消费的象征一样，砖头建筑、电力、歌剧院和自来水是金融家在20世纪初过度投资的物质享受。
到1906年旧金山地震发生时，资本从内华达州撤出，转向该市重建过程中出现的更可靠的投资机会，这一信息得到了响亮而明确的接收。内华达州人可以有利可图地从山上开采矿石，但鼓励内华达州人建造任何持久的东西都是无利可图的。
因此，除了一些与世隔绝的农业和牧场城镇，包括最终成为里诺、拉斯维加斯和埃尔科的核心地区外，我们没有这样做。
1906年后的采矿营地变成了帐篷营地，或者，如果他们特别有利可图的话，就变成了木屋的收藏品。他们中的大多数要么被烧毁，要么被冲走，要么被拆解，挨个棚屋，然后在下一个地点重新组装。虽然这是一种从内华达州沙漠中开采矿石的更有利可图的方式，但它并没有在该州建立公民参与或持久的基础设施。
第二件事-人们认为我们的恶习终究会成为好邻居-始于40多年前，当时新泽西州在美国建国200周年期间将大西洋城的赌场赌博合法化。由于新泽西州距离美国大部分人口中心更近，一位来自纽约的小型房地产开发商(也是未来的政治家)得出结论，大西洋城很有可能成为下一个拉斯维加斯-歌舞女郎、有奖斗殴等等-并相应地押注，无论是用自己的钱，还是用其他任何他能拿到的钱。
好消息是，至少对于我们这些不需要在他现在已经破产的赌场工作或生活在附近的人来说，他错了。非常非常错误。
坏消息是为什么他错了。大西洋城证明了赌场博彩没有什么特别之处-只要价格便宜，在任何地方都可以盈利。然而，娱乐业则是另一回事。大西洋城的不幸之处在于，它的竞争对手是纽约市、时代广场和百老汇，而不是拉斯维加斯。全国都有赌场赌博的市场，而且绝对存在-只是看起来更像是散布在全国各地的100家规模较小的以当地为导向的赌场，而不是距离麦迪逊广场花园两小时车程的泰姬陵(Taj Mahal)。
拉斯维加斯，乃至更广泛的克拉克县，都受益于精心策划的无耻文化(“拉斯维加斯发生的一切都留在拉斯维加斯”)和举办顶级娱乐活动的一贯历史。然而，内华达州的其他地区从来没有预算与拉斯维加斯争夺人才，也没有胃口成为自由主义者的游乐场。由于无法始终如一地为游客提供令人信服的理由，让他们在离家更近的地方旅行，而不是在离家更近的地方赌博，克拉克县以外的赌场在20世纪90年代开始衰落，就像近一个世纪前的旧采矿营地那样，慢慢蒸发和整合。
从那时起，每个领域都学会了专攻其他领域，发展出新的存在理由。然而，它们绝对没有一个能为拉斯维加斯提供一个可以效仿的有用榜样-这是一个问题，因为博彩业的康斯托克博彩(Comstock Lode)已经玩完了。谢尔顿·阿德尔森能感觉到。米高梅度假村也可以。是的，新冠肺炎可能是引发这一下跌的催化剂，就像1906年旧金山地震是将内华达州的矿山推到边缘的催化剂一样，但现在已经有一段时间了。自2016年以来，游客数量一直在下降，机场客流量直到2017年才最终超过2007年的客流量，十多年来，通胀增长一直快于博彩收入的增长。
换句话说，早在新冠肺炎出现之前，拉斯维加斯就陷入了困境。病毒刚刚暴露了问题所在。
即使你不住在克拉克县(我现在不住在克拉克县，也从来没有住过克拉克县)，也不可能忽视住在那里的75%的内华达州人，以及内华达州最大的工业倒闭导致的州政府预算缺口。内华达州其他地区在持续与高于平均水平的失业率作斗争的同时，无论是在人口统计学上还是在经济上，都没有足够的数字来有意义地支持南内华达州。
不幸的是，该州其他地区也没有为克拉克县提供很多令人信服的道路可循。
例如，拉斯维加斯永远不会是太浩湖。首先，米德湖几乎没有足够的水，风景也远没有那么风景如画(尽管我坦率地承认，红岩和查尔斯顿山各有其美)。同样，除非克拉克县愿意将人口减少到目前人口的百分之一，并开始在附近的每一个山脉进行露天开采，否则埃尔科可能也不会提供太多有用的例子。
这就剩下了雷诺和斯帕克斯，它们似乎是更中肯的例子。瓦肖县的50万居民在规模上更接近克拉克县的200万和变化。这两个城市都靠近加州的大都市地区。瓦肖县甚至是内华达州最初的博彩和旅游圣地。如果里诺和斯帕克斯能够通过多元化和生产来摆脱其旅游经济的衰退，为什么拉斯维加斯不能呢？
首先，克拉克县的人口比瓦肖县多得多，对博彩业的依赖程度也一直比瓦肖县高得多，几万人的再培训和再就业比几十万人的再培训和再就业要容易得多。
其次，雷诺和斯帕克斯靠近一个几十年前几乎完全去工业化的大都市地区，他们选择专门从事技术和编码。另一方面，洛杉矶不是旧金山-相反，它是美国最大的制造业中心，它与美国最大和第二大集装箱港口(洛杉矶和长滩港)相连。
为了让雷诺、斯帕克斯和附近的社区有效地与旧金山湾区争夺制造业就业机会，他们不仅仅依靠较低的税收。例如，霍桑的销售税和财产税比内华达州的大部分地区要低得多，但你看不到企业急于迁往那里。网络效应-熟悉制造业工作的潜在员工、附近的供应商等等-也很重要，以及对劳动力的竞争。由于湾区在几十年前就停止了有意义的制造，转而选择专注于金融和技术，因此北内华达州并不是在与旧金山湾区现有的制造商网络竞争，这些网络拥有满足现有地区需求的经验和能力。
然而，在拉斯维加斯的情况下，制造业中的所有网络效应都指向错误的方向。几代人以来，洛杉矶一直是一个工业强国，拥有支持甚至比它已经产生的更多工业产出的交通基础设施。与雷诺(Reno)和星火(Spark)不同的是，这两家公司可以就近竞争，争取湾区公司寻求在轻工制造领域探索机会，而拉斯维加斯必须与距离洛杉矶老牌制造商更远，以及洛杉矶已经在做的工作中熟练工人更少的情况下竞争。
顺便说一句，这也是为什么拉斯维加斯没有变成另一个硅谷的原因，尽管那里经常试图培育一个硅谷。克拉克县从来没有大量的本土编程人才，UNLV也没有培养出足够的程序员来支持他们。另一方面，洛杉矶已经有了大量的程序员(最初是由国防承包商等培养出来的)，以及培养他们的发达的高等教育体系-这就是为什么洛杉矶现在有硅滩，而拉斯维加斯则在网上销售鞋子。
我提起这件事是为了不给克拉克县的游行队伍添麻烦--尽管天气一直很干燥，但如果我提出来，你会很感激的。如果克拉克县不想成为沙漠中的底特律，经济多元化绝对是至关重要的，制造业无疑将在其中发挥作用。然而，正如弗吉尼亚州自从康斯托克洛德(Comstock Lode)耗尽后再也不是以前的样子一样，拉斯维加斯也有可能永远不会是曾经的拉斯维加斯。如果是这样的话，我们最好学会如何适应这一点。
大卫·科尔本(David Colborne)在自由党活跃了20年。在此期间，他断断续续地在自己的个人博客和内华达州自由党博客上写博客，并以自由党候选人的身份两次竞选公职。他在他所在的州和县自由党分会担任执行委员会成员。他是两个儿子的父亲，是一名IT专业人士。你可以在Twitter@DavidColborne上关注他，或者给他发电子邮件到[电子邮件受保护]。

note_en:
Traffic streaks along Las Vegas Boulevard on Monday, March 20, 2017. (Jeff Scheid/The Nevada Independent)
   The last time I wrote  a column immediately following our state’s birthday, I kept things relatively light — and why not? The weather was fine. My fianceé and I went for a run in Carson City. My friend and his new wife brought his dog to the Nevada Day parade. Both I and our now-governor pet his dog, albeit not at the same time. Ryan Bundy, running for governor himself, rode by in a homemade armored personnel carrier.
 Things were, in a word, normal. Yes, there was clearly room for what passed for “normal” in Nevada at the time, but there always is, there always was, and there always will be.
 Being a Nevadan has always meant buying into something of a paradox. Culturally, we like to pride ourselves on our independence and self-reliance. In reality, both of our largest metropolitan areas and our state capital are all watered from watersheds that originate from out-of-state; we produce less food than any state that  isn’t part of New England; and we produce less  manufacturing output than  Idaho (not per capita — overall), to say nothing of the  rest  of  our  neighbors.
  This isn’t necessarily a bad thing. Due to Nevada’s perpetually parched landscape, our state produces  more food than it should and would produce less if  we were legally allowed to. Manufacturing can get thirsty, too, as  recent  attempts to secure water for two of our state’s largest industrial parks revealed. Consequently, given our resource constraints, it makes sense that Nevadans would choose to do one thing well — usually something other states are more reluctant to countenance within their borders — over trying to embrace the routine industriousness that propels most other state economies.
 What we’ve lacked in natural resources, we’ve historically tried to make up in shamelessness and single-mindedness.
 Unfortunately, as Nevadans have been forced to learn the hard way over and over again throughout our state's 156 years, doing one thing nobody else wants to do, and doing it well, is all fine and good until one of two things happens — either people decide they want what we’ve hyper-specialized upon less than they used to, or, inspired by our example, they decide what we’re offering isn’t so bad for their backyards after all.
 The first happened to mining over a century ago. In the 19th century, the Comstock Lode — which maintained heavy and steady production for nearly two decades — set what turned out to be grossly unrealistic expectations for other mining operations throughout Nevada. There was enough ore underground to produce more than a few millionaires and build a town with all of the modern conveniences the time had to offer from scratch. With that sole example in mind, financiers in San Francisco, flush with California gold and Comstock silver, were more than happy to sink capital into town building in exchange for being the first at the next site of the next gold or silver rush.
 Too bad there wasn’t one — not at the Comstock’s scale, anyway.
 Towns like Tonopah, Goldfield, and Rhyolite proved to be the dot-com busts of the early 20th century. Each of them began with world-beating ambition and built creature comforts accordingly, only to discover they were each sitting on a few years of ore at most. Even Virginia City, which started the city-building trend in the first place, already began its decline. In much the same way ping-pong tables and in-office catering became symbols of Silicon Valley excess in the late ‘90s, brick construction, electricity, opera houses and running water were the creature comforts financiers overinvested in during the early 1900’s.
 By the time the 1906 San Francisco quake struck, which drew capital out of Nevada and towards more reliable investment opportunities presented in the rebuilding of the city, the message was received loud and clear. Nevadans could profitably pull ore out of mountains, but there was no profit in encouraging Nevadans to build anything to last.
 So, outside of a few isolated farming and ranching towns, including the nuclei of what would eventually become Reno, Las Vegas, and Elko, we didn’t.
 Post-1906 mining camps became tent camps, or, if they were especially profitable, collections of wooden shacks. Most of them either burned down, washed away, or were disassembled, shack by shack, and reassembled at the next site. Though this was a considerably more profitable way to extract ore from Nevada’s deserts, it did nothing to build civic engagement or lasting infrastructure in the state.
 The second thing — people deciding our vices make good neighbors after all — started more than four decades ago when New Jersey legalized casino gambling in Atlantic City during the nation’s bicentennial. Owing to New Jersey’s closer proximity to most of the nation’s population centers, a minor real estate developer (and future politician) from New York concluded that Atlantic City had an excellent chance at becoming the next Las Vegas — showgirls, prize fights, and all — and bet accordingly, both with his money and anyone else’s he could get his hands on.
 The good news, at least for those of us who don’t have to work in or live near his now-bankrupt casinos, was that  he was wrong. Very,   very wrong.
 The bad news is  why he was wrong. Atlantic City demonstrated that there was nothing special about casino gaming — it could be profitable anywhere as long as it’s done cheap. Entertainment, however, is a different story. Atlantic City’s misfortune was that it was competing against New York City, Times Square and Broadway, not Las Vegas. There was and absolutely is a market for casino gambling throughout the country — it just looks more like a hundred smaller locally oriented casinos scattered throughout the country than a Taj Mahal two hours from Madison Square Garden competing for the same acts.
  Las Vegas, and Clark County more broadly, has benefited from both a carefully curated culture of shamelessness (“What happens in Las Vegas stays in Las Vegas”) and a consistent history of hosting top-shelf entertainment. The rest of Nevada, however, never had the budget to compete with Las Vegas for talent nor the stomach to become an amusement park for libertines. Unable to provide consistently compelling reasons for tourists to travel instead of gambling closer to home, casinos outside of Clark County started declining in the 1990s, slowly evaporating and consolidating as the old mining camps did nearly a century prior.
 Since then, each area has learned to specialize in something else, to develop a new reason for existence. Absolutely none of them, however, provide Las Vegas a useful example to follow — and that’s a problem because the Comstock Lode of gambling is played out. Sheldon Adelson  can feel it. So can  MGM Resorts. Yes, COVID-19 might be the catalyst that sparked this decline, just as the 1906 San Francisco earthquake was the catalyst that pushed Nevada’s mines over the edge, but the writing has been on the wall for a while now.  Visitor counts have been declining since 2016, airport traffic only finally exceeded 2007 traffic in 2017, and inflation has been growing faster than gaming revenue for more than a decade.
 Las Vegas, in other words, was in trouble long before COVID-19 came along. The virus just revealed where the problems were.
  Even if you don’t live in Clark County (which I don’t and never have), it’s impossible to ignore the 75 percent of Nevadans who do live there and the gaping hole in the state government’s budget caused by the collapse of Nevada’s largest industry. The rest of Nevada simply doesn’t have the numbers — neither demographically nor economically — to meaningfully support Southern Nevada while it persistently struggles with  higher-than-average unemployment.
 Unfortunately, the rest of the state also doesn’t offer many compelling paths for Clark County to follow, either.
 Las Vegas, for example, will never be Lake Tahoe. There’s not nearly enough water in Lake Mead, for starters, nor is the scenery anywhere near that picturesque (though I’ll freely concede that Red Rock and Mount Charleston are pretty in their own way). Similarly, unless Clark County is willing to reduce its population to a hundredth of its current population and start strip mining every nearby mountain range, Elko probably won’t provide many useful examples, either.
 This leaves Reno and Sparks, which seem like more pertinent examples. The half a million residents in Washoe County is closer in scale to the two million and change in Clark County. Both are near large California metropolitan areas. Washoe County was even Nevada’s original gaming and tourism mecca. If Reno and Sparks can diversify and manufacture their way out of the decline of their tourist economies, why can’t Las Vegas?
  First, Clark County’s population is significantly larger and has always been considerably more dependent upon gaming employment than Washoe County’s. It’s easier to retrain and re-employ tens of thousands of workers than it is to retrain and re-employ hundreds of thousands of workers.
 Second, Reno and Sparks are close to a metropolitan area that almost completely deindustrialized decades ago, choosing instead to specialize in technology and coding. Los Angeles, on the other hand, isn’t San Francisco — instead, it’s the  largest manufacturing center in the nation and it’s connected to the nation’s largest and second-largest container ports (the ports of Los Angeles and Long Beach).
 In order for Reno, Sparks, and nearby communities to effectively compete against the Bay Area for manufacturing jobs, they don’t just rely upon lower taxes. Sales and property taxes, for example, are considerably lower in Hawthorne than most of Nevada, yet you don’t see businesses eager to relocate there. Network effects — potential employees familiar with manufacturing work, nearby suppliers, and so on —- also matter, as well as competition for labor. Since the Bay Area stopped meaningfully manufacturing much of anything decades ago, choosing to focus on finance and technology instead, Northern Nevada isn’t competing against pre-existing networks of manufacturers in the Bay Area with the experience and ability to meet existing regional needs.
 In the case of Las Vegas, however, all of the network effects in manufacturing point in the wrong direction. Los Angeles has been an industrial powerhouse for generations and has the transportation infrastructure to support even more industrial output than it already generates. Unlike Reno and Sparks, which can compete on proximity for Bay Area companies looking to explore opportunities in light manufacturing, Las Vegas has to compete against being both farther away from established manufacturers in Los Angeles and having fewer workers skilled in the work Los Angeles is already doing.
 This, incidentally, is also why Las Vegas hasn’t turned into another Silicon Valley, despite periodic attempts to nurture one there. Clark County never had a large native population of programming talent, nor does UNLV produce enough programmers to support one. Los Angeles, on the other hand, already had plenty of programmers (originally nurtured by defense contractors, among others) and a well-developed post-secondary education system that produces them — which is why Los Angeles now has  Silicon Beach while Las Vegas sells shoes online.
 I bring this up not to rain on Clark County’s parade — though, as dry as it’s been, you might appreciate it down there if I did. Diversifying Clark County’s economy is absolutely vital if it doesn’t want to become Detroit in the desert and manufacturing will undoubtedly play a part in it. However, just as Virginia City has never been the same since the Comstock Lode exhausted itself, it’s possible Las Vegas will never be what it once was, either. If so, we better learn how to get comfortable with that.
  David Colborne has been active in the Libertarian Party for two decades. During that time, he has blogged intermittently on his personal blog, as well as the Libertarian Party of Nevada blog, and ran for office twice as a Libertarian candidate. He serves on the Executive Committee for both his state and county Libertarian Party chapters. He is the father of two sons and an IT professional. You can follow him on Twitter @DavidColborne or email him at    [email protected] .

赫克特在一份声明中说：“对于小企业主来说，那里可能是狂野的西部。”他说：“寻找创业、发展和资助企业所需的金融产品和指导可能非常具有挑战性，而且大多数小企业主在创业过程中没有资源或合作伙伴为他们提供支持。为这一过程带来透明度，并为企业主提供教育、授权和倡导，这与我们在消费者领域看到的NerdWallet所做的非常相似。“。
当然，考虑到大流行的影响，小企业现在可能特别迫切需要援助。
根据公告，Fundera将作为NerdWallet的子公司运营，整个团队将进行过渡。目标是帮助NerdWallet通过内容和实际融资两方面向中小企业市场扩张。
NerdWallet联合创始人兼首席执行官蒂姆·陈表示：“虽然我们提供免费工具和内容，但我们从未能够完全支持小企业主-这一点在今天发生了变化。”Fundera几年来一直是我们的合作伙伴之一，他们对中小企业市场的深刻理解，他们与贷款人和企业主建立的长期、值得信赖的关系，以及他们将小企业主的需求放在首位的承诺，确实是独一无二的，令人印象深刻。“。
此次收购的财务条款没有披露。据Crunchbase称，Fundera已从QED Investors、Khosla Ventures、First圆形Capital和Susquehanna Growth Equity等投资者那里筹集了1,890万美元的资金。
这是NerdWallet在2020年的第二次收购，此前他曾收购了总部位于英国的Know Your Money。该公司表示，在过去的几年里，它一直在增长并实现盈利。
note_en:
“It can be the wild wild west out there for small business owners,” Hecht said in a statement. “Finding the financial products and the guidance needed to start, grow and fund their businesses can be very challenging, and most small business owners don’t have a resource or partner to support them along their journey. Bringing transparency to this process and educating, empowering and advocating for business owners is so similar to what we see  NerdWallet   doing in the consumer space.”
 And of course, small businesses may be in particularly dire need of assistance now, given the impact of the pandemic.
  According to  the announcement, Fundera will operate as a subsidiary of NerdWallet, with the entire team making the transition. The goal is to help NerdWallet expand into the small- and medium-business market with both content and actual financing.
 “Although we offer free tools and content, we’ve never been able to fully support small business owners — that changes today,” said NerdWallet co-founder and CEO Tim Chen. “Fundera has been one of our partners for several years and their deep understanding of the SMB market, the long-standing, trusted relationships they’ve built with both lenders and business owners, and their commitment to putting the needs of small business owners first is really unique and impressive.”
 The financial terms of the acquisition were not disclosed. Fundera had raised $18.9 million in funding from investors including QED Investors, Khosla Ventures, First Round Capital and Susquehanna Growth Equity,  according to Crunchbase.
 This is NerdWallet’s second acquisition of 2020, having  previously acquired U.K.-based Know Your Money. The company says it’s been growing and profitable for the past several years.
title: NerdWallet收购小企业贷款市场Fundera
title_en: NerdWallet acquires small business loan marketplace Fundera

X的研究人员希望，抑郁症和其他疾病和障碍一样，可能会有一个明确的生物标志物，这将有助于医疗专业人员更容易、更客观地诊断抑郁症，这也有望使其更容易和更稳定地可治疗。通过在实验室中使用专门为此目的设计的游戏进行的研究，在脑电图方面，有过一些先例，在这些游戏中，抑郁症患者似乎总是表现出较低的脑电图活动，以回应有效地“赢得”游戏。
这些研究似乎提供了一条通往潜在生物标记物的途径，但为了让它们在现实世界的诊断环境中真正有用，比如诊所或公共卫生实验室，X的团队开始改进脑电收集和解释的过程，使其更容易为用户和技术人员所用。
Alphabet今天发布的这篇详细介绍其努力的帖子中，最值得注意的可能是，这本质上是一个长达一年的调查没有结果的故事--而不是你通常从大型科技公司听到的登月故事的一面。
事实上，这可能是迄今为止最好的例子之一，说明了大型科技公司的许多方法的批评者未能理解的是，在软件和工程领域，一些问题不是通过类似的解决方案就能解决的。X的团队将他们从这个长达数年的研究项目中学到的东西总结成了关于用户研究的三个主要要点，每一个要点都在某种程度上触及了纯粹的客观生物标记物检测方法的不足(即使它已经奏效)，特别是在精神疾病方面。来自研究人员：
心理健康测量仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。尽管有许多心理健康调查和量表可用，但它们并没有被广泛使用，特别是在初级保健和咨询环境中。原因从负担(“我没有时间做这个”)到怀疑(“使用量表并不比使用我的临床判断好”)，再到缺乏信任(“我认为我的客户没有如实填写”和“我不想向我的顾问透露这么多”)。这些发现与以测量为基础的精神卫生保健的文献是一致的。任何新的测量工具都必须通过为有生活经验的人和临床医生创造明确的价值来克服这些障碍。
将主观和客观数据结合起来是有价值的。有生活经验的人和临床医生都欢迎引入客观指标，但不是作为主观评估的替代，也不是询问人们他们的经历和感受。主观和客观指标的结合被视为特别强大。客观的衡量标准可能会验证主观体验；或者，如果两者不同，这本身就是一个有趣的洞察力，为对话提供了起点。
以下是新测量技术的多个使用案例。我们最初的假设是，临床医生可能会使用“脑波测试”作为诊断辅助手段。然而，这一概念得到了冷淡的回应。精神健康专家，如精神病学家和临床心理学家，对他们通过临床访谈进行诊断的能力感到有信心。初级保健医生认为脑电图测试可能有用，但前提是在咨询患者之前由医疗助理进行，类似于血压测试。辅导员和社会工作者在执业过程中不做诊断，所以与他们无关。一些有生活经验的人不喜欢被机器贴上抑郁的标签。相比之下，人们对使用技术作为持续监测的工具-捕捉一段时间内精神健康状态的变化-以了解两次访问之间发生的事情有着明显的浓厚兴趣。许多临床医生询问他们是否可以将EEG系统送回家，这样他们的患者和客户就可以自己重复测试。他们对脑电图潜在的预测能力也非常感兴趣，例如预测未来谁可能变得更抑郁。需要更多的研究来确定如何将脑电图等工具最好地部署在临床和咨询环境中，包括如何将其与其他测量技术(如数字表型分析)相结合。
X正在将Amber的硬件和软件在Github上开源，并发布了一项“专利承诺”，以确保X不会通过使用开源材料对与Amber相关的EEG专利的用户提起任何法律诉讼。目前还不清楚(虽然不太可能)，如果Amber成功地找到了抑郁症的单一生物标记物，结果会不会是这样，但也许在更广泛的社区手中，研究小组所做的使脑电图更容易获得的工作，将导致其他有趣的发现。
note_en:
X’s researchers were hoping that depression, like other ailments and disorders, might have a clear biomarker that would help healthcare professionals more easily and objectively diagnose depression, which would also then hopefully make it more easily and consistency treatable. With EEG, there was some precedent, via studies done in labs using games designed specifically for the purpose, in which people with depression seemed to consistently demonstrate a lower measure of EEG activity in response to effectively ‘winning’ the games.
 These studies seemed to offer a path to a potential biomarker, but in order to make them actually useful in real-world diagnostic settings, like a clinic or a public health lab, the team at X set about improving the process of EEG collection and interpretation to make it more accessible, both to users and to technicians.
  What is perhaps most notable about this pursuit, and the post today that Alphabet released detailing its efforts, is that it’s essentially a story of a years-long investigation that didn’t work out – not the side of the moonshot story you typically hear from big tech companies.
 In fact, this is perhaps one of the best examples yet of what critics of many of the approaches of large tech companies fail to understand – that some problems are not solvable by solutions with analogs in the world of software and engineering. The team at X sums its learning’s from the years-long research project up in three main bullet point about its user research, and each of them touch in some way on the insufficiency of a pure objective biomarker detection method (even if it had worked) particularly when it comes to mental illness. From the researchers:
 Mental health measurement remains an unsolved problem. Despite the availability of many mental health surveys and scales, they are not widely used, especially in primary care and counseling settings. Reasons range from burden (“I don’t have time for this”) to skepticism (“Using a scale is no better than using my clinical judgement”) to lack of trust (“I don’t think my client is filling this in truthfully” and ”I don’t want to reveal this much to my counsellor”). These findings were in line with the literature on measurement-based mental health care. Any new measurement tool would have to overcome these barriers by creating clear value for both the person with lived experience and the clinician.
 There is value in combining subjective and objective data. People with lived experience and clinicians both welcomed the introduction of objective metrics, but not as a replacement for subjective assessment and asking people about their experience and feelings. The combination of subjective and objective metrics was seen as especially powerful. Objective metrics might validate the subjective experience; or if the two diverge, that in itself is an interesting insight which provides the starting point for a conversation.
 T here are multiple use cases for new measurement technology. Our initial hypothesis was that clinicians might use a “brainwave test” as a diagnostic aid. However, this concept got a lukewarm reception. Mental health experts such as psychiatrists and clinical psychologists felt confident in their ability to diagnose via clinical interview. Primary care physicians thought an EEG test could be useful, but only if it was conducted by a medical assistant before their consultation with the patient, similar to a blood pressure test. Counsellors and social workers don’t do diagnosis in their practice, so it was irrelevant to them. Some people with lived experience did not like the idea of being labelled as depressed by a machine. By contrast, there was a notably strong interest in using technology as a tool for ongoing monitoring — capturing changes in mental health state over time — to learn what happens between visits. Many clinicians asked if they could send the EEG system home so their patients and clients could repeat the test on their own. They were also very interested in EEG’s potential predictive qualities, e.g. predicting who is likely to get more depressed in future. More research is needed to determine how a tool such as EEG would be best deployed in clinical and counseling settings, including how it could be combined with other measurement technologies such as digital phenotyping.
 X is making Amber’ hardware and software open-source on Github, and also issuing a ‘patent pledge’ that ensures X will not bring any legal action against users of the EEG Patents related to Amber through use of the open-sourced material. It’s unclear (though unlikely) that this would’ve been the result had Amber succeeded at finding a single biomarker for depression, but perhaps in the hands of the broader community the work the team did on rendering EEG more accessible beyond specialized testing facilities will lead to other interesting discoveries.
title: Alphabet的X详细介绍了琥珀项目，这是一项为抑郁症寻找单一生物标记物的探索，但没有达到目标
title_en: Alphabet’s X details Project Amber, a quest for a single biomarker for depression that fell short of its goal

note:
肖恩·康纳利(Sean Connery)周六去世后，一封写给苹果联合创始人史蒂夫·乔布斯(Steve Jobs)的假信在社交媒体上流传。我只想重申一遍：这是假的，是幽默网站Scoopertino的产品，该网站发布关于苹果公司和库比蒂诺(了解它的名字)总部发生的事情的讽刺文章。
这封日期为1998年的打印信据称表明了康纳利对乔布斯要求他出现在苹果广告中的愤怒。“我不会为苹果或任何其他公司出卖灵魂。我对你建议的‘改变世界’没有兴趣，“它写道。“你是个电脑推销员，我在操詹姆斯·邦德！”
但互联网喜欢太好而不真实的东西，周日早上，这封“信”在Twitter上流传。
乔恩，我尊重你的观点，但请看看揭穿这封信的研究报告。好人散布假货会伤害我们所有人。Https://t.co/IHNO8bvPX7。
-史蒂文·利维(@StevenLevy)2020年11月1日。
所以，这听起来确实像康纳利可能说过/打过的东西，但我认为第一个泄露的是字母右下角的小“007”水印。康纳利多年来一直试图摆脱詹姆斯·邦德的束缚，到1998年，他已经转向其他角色。
正如我们在CNET的朋友们指出的那样，这封假信也在2011年疯传，这要归功于另一位毫无戒心的Twitter用户。
就像互联网上的任何东西一样，永远记住要信任但要核实。在这种情况下，相信这封“信”是一封恶搞-并赞扬你在Scoopertino这里所做的任何SEO魔术，因为它显然是常青树的内容。这是我们在互联网行业中通常喜欢的，但只有在它是真实的时候才会这样。
note_en:
A fake letter from Sean Connery to Apple co-founder Steve Jobs is making the rounds on social media following the  actor’s death on Saturday. Just to reiterate: it’s fake, the product of humor site Scoopertino, which posts satirical articles about Apple and goings-on at its Cupertino (get it that’s the name) headquarters.
 The typewritten letter dated 1998 purports to show Connery’s outrage over Jobs asking him to appear in an Apple commercial. “I do not sell my soul for Apple or any other company. I have no interest in ‘changing the world’ as you suggest,” it states. “You are a computer salesman, I am fucking JAMES BOND!”
 But the internet loves things that are too good to be true, and Sunday morning, the “letter” was circulating on Twitter.
 Jon, I respect your views but please look at the research debunking this letter. It hurts us all when good people circulate fakes.  https://t.co/IHNO8bvPX7
— Steven Levy (@StevenLevy)  November 1, 2020
 So, it does sound like something Connery might have said/typed, but I think the first giveaway is the little “007” watermark in the lower right corner of the letter. Connery tried for years to break free of James Bond, and by 1998 he had moved on to other roles.
 And as our friends at  CNet noted, the fake letter also went viral in 2011 thanks to another unsuspecting Twitter user.
 As with anything on the internet, always remember to trust but verify. In this case, trust that this “letter” was a parody— and kudos on whatever SEO magic you did here Scoopertino, because it’s clearly evergreen content. Which we usually love in the internet business, but only when it’s true.
title: 不，肖恩·康纳利没有写一封刻薄的信给史蒂夫·乔布斯
title_en: No, Sean Connery Did Not Write a Mean Letter To Steve Jobs

note:
NHS COVID应用程序的一个错误可能让数千人没有意识到他们接触了冠状病毒，需要自我隔离。
自9月24日以来，已有1900多万人下载了这款英文接触者追踪应用程序，但它的设置错误，无法记录这些人之间的距离是否足够近，足以传播冠状病毒。
周日，迈克尔·戈夫告诉索菲里奇，英格兰长达一个月的禁赛可能会延长到12月2日之后。
据“星期日泰晤士报”最先报道，由于该应用程序为记录人与人之间的联系设置了错误的风险阈值，收到潜在暴露警告的用户数量低得惊人。
虽然这项技术本应识别出距离足够近的人，以便进行传播，但它却记录下了他们距离太远，病毒不能在他们之间传播。
天空新闻已经独立证实了这一问题，并了解到很大一部分关于暴露的幻影通知-卫生部告诉用户忽略这些通知-实际上是真实的。
根据“星期日泰晤士报”的消息来源，Android设备受到的影响最大，这意味着这个错误对低收入智能手机用户造成了不成比例的影响。
与此同时，那些被联系人追踪应用程序告知隔离的人无法申请政府的财政支持，这引发了人们的担忧，即低薪工人将被迫在健康和困难之间做出选择。
这个问题在上周的一篇博客文章中首次得到承认，该文章证实，尽管开发人员知道风险门槛需要低于最初设计的风险门槛，但在全国发布之前没有更新。
政府没有透露有多少英格兰人被这款应用指示自我孤立，但苏格兰和北爱尔兰的权力下放已经允许当局分别联系1万人和1.6万人。
英国卫生与社会保障部的一位发言人表示，这款应用是世界上唯一一款使用谷歌-苹果最新技术来更好地测量距离以识别风险最高的人的应用，按照国际标准，这款应用被认为是优秀的。
正如之前发布的那样，我们预计会有更多感染病毒的高风险应用程序用户收到自我隔离的通知，这将降低病毒携带者将病毒传染给其他人的机会，从而对每个人都有长期好处。
在经历了一个艰难的开始后，这款联系人追踪应用程序似乎已经站稳了脚跟，以一定的速度攀升到了1900万次下载量。就在上周，卫生和社会保健部的一位资深消息人士告诉我，他们对这一进展感到非常高兴。
这一最新消息令人质疑这一进展。如果这个应用程序发送的警报太少，是因为它的风险阈值被错误地设置得太高，那么它提供了什么保护呢？不是没有：已经发送了一些警报。但在迫切需要保护的时候，这款应用程序并没有尽到自己的责任。
我知道这个软件错误有一个令人难以置信的人为因素。风险门槛本打算在发射前改变，但由于某种原因，没有人做出关键的更新。几个星期以来，测试和跟踪一直在进行，就好像改变已经进行了一样。
这些事情当然会发生，但在这款应用程序中，它们已经发生了很多次。发射两周后，它只发出了一次关于场地的警报。任何被要求隔离的人即使有权获得收入支持，也不能申请收入支持。
这一消息也让来自测试和跟踪的通信受到了质疑。几周来，人们一直在抱怨这款应用给他们发来的信息，告诉他们和新冠肺炎在一起的人很近。首先，卫生部说没有问题，然后发布了一个修复程序，告诉人们不用担心。
事实证明，由于这个错误，这些警报中的许多应该是指示人们隔离的。
在病毒迅速传播的时候，这是一个重大的错误。我知道马特·汉考克和迪多·哈丁和其他人一样对此一无所知。尽管如此，当这类错误不断发生时，人们可能会质疑他们的领导力。
note_en:
An error with the NHS COVID app has left potentially thousands of people unaware that they were exposed to the coronavirus and needed to self-isolate.
 More than 19 million people have downloaded the English contact-tracing app since 24 September, but it has had the wrong settings to record whether these people were close enough to each other to transmit the   coronavirus.
  The revelation comes as Michael Gove told Sophy Ridge on Sunday that England's month-long lockdown   could be extended beyond 2 December.
   "Shockingly low" numbers of users have been sent warnings about potential exposures due to the app having the wrong risk threshold set for recording contacts between people, as The Sunday Times first reported.
 While the technology should have been recognising people as having been in close enough proximity for a transmission, it had instead recorded that they were too far away for the virus to be passed between them.
   Sky News has independently confirmed the issue, and also learnt that a large proportion of the   phantom notifications about exposures - which the Department for Health told users to ignore - were actually real.
  According to The Sunday Times' source, Android devices have been the worst impacted - meaning the error has had a disproportionate impact on low-income smartphone owners.
  It comes as those who have been told to isolate by the contact-tracing app are   not able to claim government financial support, raising fears that low-paid workers will be forced to choose between health and hardship.
 The issue was first admitted in a blog post last week which confirmed that although the developers knew the risk threshold needed to be lower than had been originally designed, it had not been updated ahead of the national release.
 The government has not disclosed how many people in England have been instructed to self-isolate by the app, but devolved administrations of Scotland and Northern Ireland have allowed authorities to contact 10,000 and 16,000 people respectively.
   A spokesperson for the Department for Health and Social Care said its app was "the only app in the world using the latest Google-Apple technology to better gauge distance to identify those most at risk, and is deemed 'excellent' by international standards.
 "As previously published, we anticipate more app users who are at high risk of having caught the virus will receive a notification to self-isolate, and that will be to everyone's long term benefit by reducing the chances of those with the virus passing it on to others."
   After a troubled start, the contact tracing app seemed to have found its feet, climbing with some speed to 19 million downloads. Just last week, a senior Department of Health and Social Care source told me they were "very pleased" with its progress.
 This latest news calls that progress into question. If the app was sending far too few alerts because its risk threshold was incorrectly set too high, then what protection did it offer? Not none: some alerts were sent. But at a time when protection was desperately needed, the app was not pulling its weight.
 I understand that this software error had an incredibly human source. The risk threshold was meant to be changed before the launch but for some reason no-one one made the crucial update. For weeks, Test and Trace carried on as if the change had gone ahead.
 Of course these things happen, but in the case of the app they have happened an awful lot. Two weeks after launch it had only sent one alert about a venue. Anyone asked to isolate by it   still cannot claim income support even if they are entitled to it.
 The news also calls into question the communications from test and trace. For weeks people have been complaining about messages from the app telling them they've been near someone with covid-19. First, the Department of Health said there wasn't an issue, then it released a fix telling people not to worry.
 It turns out that because of this error, many of those alerts should have been instructing people to isolate.
 Coming at a time when the virus was spreading rapidly, this is a significant error. I understand Matt Hancock and Dido Harding were as much in the dark about this as anyone. Still, questions may be asked about their leadership when mistakes of this kind keep happening.
title: 资料来源：英国的NHS COVID应用程序，下载量为1900万次，风险阈值错误，并向“令人震惊的低”用户发出关于潜在接触的警告
title_en: Sources: UK's NHS COVID app, downloaded 19M times, had the wrong risk threshold and sent warnings to a “shockingly low” number of users about potential exposure

3年前，我开始了一个项目，名为“铁锈般的着色语言”(Rust Like Shading Language)，将铁锈带到GPU上。我对GLSL和HLSL的现状非常不满，我想象如果我们有适当的工具，如格式化、编译器错误、适当的类型系统、包管理器、自动完成等，着色器编程将会是什么样子。在Vulkan引入SPIR-V之后，我期待着许多新的着色语言的出现，但没有一种是真正的，我决定自己解决问题。
对编译器一无所知，我为一种非常简单的语言编写了一个简单的解析器和类型检查器，我可以生成一个三角形，但我意识到编写自己的编译器是一项相当艰巨的任务，我正在考虑使用rustc。
经过几天的挠头之后，我可以再次绘制一个三角形，从铁锈-&gt；hir(高级IR)-&gt；SPIR-V开始。虽然将HIR转换为SPIR-V很简单，但也很耗时。我决定试用MIR(中级IR)。经过几周的摸索之后，我终于可以再次渲染这个三角形了，几天后，我还可以从shadertoy生成默认的着色器。
我预计会遇到严重的阻止问题。虽然肯定会有一些挑战，但我总是能找到一种方法来绕过它们。我只是不停地添加功能：
到那个时候，我正在处理稍微复杂的着色器，比如光线行进，然后我遇到了一个小小的凹陷。当时的生态系统相当不成熟，调试错误的编解码器非常耗时。我真的需要一种方法来自动化调试过程。
我也是一名大学生，我在RLSL上花了太多的时间，这超出了我应该花的时间。与专注于学习相比，我真的很难证明我在RLSL上的工作是合理的。
几个月后，我在登船工作室看到一篇关于生锈的帖子。长话短说，我申请了，得到了工作，从德国搬到瑞典(斯德哥尔摩)。工作中的每个人都对将Rust引入GPU非常感兴趣。只是现在还不是进行专业研究的合适时机。
在业余时间做RLSL和ASH的同时，我也在努力保持工作和生活的良好平衡。
快进一年半，我们现在有了铁锈图形处理器。有关更多细节，请参阅公告。Rust-GPU将取代RLSL。Rust-GPU是由我的一些非常有才华的同事编写的，我对他们所做的工作感到非常高兴。我正在与团队协作，尽管我现在并没有积极提交代码。我非常兴奋，很快就可以在铁锈中编写着色器了，我希望你也是。
我要感谢你们所有人多年来对我的支持。我无法告诉你，所有这些善意的评论在多大程度上帮助我度过了一些艰难的时期。谢谢你，❤️。
note_en:
3 years ago I started a project called "Rust Like Shading Language" to bring Rust to the GPU. I was very unhappy with the current state of affairs of glsl and hlsl, and I imagined what shader programming would be like if we had proper tooling like formatting, compiler errors, proper type system, package manager, auto completion etc. After Vulkan introduced SPIR-V, I was anticipating the appearance of many new shading languages, but nothing really did and I decided to take the matters in my own hand.
 Knowing nothing about compilers, I wrote a simple parser and type checker for a very simple language, and I could generate a triangle   But I realized that writing your own compiler is quite the undertaking and I was thinking about using rustc instead.
 After a few days of head scratching I could render a triangle again going from  Rust -> HIR (High level IR) -> SPIR-V. While translating HIR to SPIR-V was simple, it was also time consuming. I decided to try out MIR (Mid level IR). After a few weeks of head scratching I finally could render the triangle again, and a few days later I could also generate the default shader from shadertoy.
  I expected to run into major blocking issues. While there were certainly some challenges, I always found a way to hack around them. And I just kept on adding features constantly:
   By that time I was working on slightly more complex shaders like raymarching, and I ran into a small slump. The ecosystem was quite immature at that time and debugging incorrect codegen was time consuming. I really needed a way to automate the debugging process.
 I was also a University student, and I spent way too much time working on RLSL than I should have. I had a really hard time justifying my work on RLSL compared to focusing on my studies.
 A few months later, I saw a post from  Embark Studios about Rust. Long story short, I applied, got the Job, moved from Germany to Sweden (Stockholm). Everyone at work was really interested in bringing Rust to the GPU as well. It just wasn't the right time to work on it professionally.
 I also struggled to have a good work life balance while working on RLSL and  ash in my spare time.
  Fast forwarding 1.5 years, we now have  rust-gpu. See the  announcement for more details.  rust-gpu will replace  RLSL.  rust-gpu is written by some extremely talented colleagues of mine, and I couldn't be happier with the work they have done. I am collaborating with the team although I am not actively committing code right now. I am extremely excited to be writing shaders in Rust soon and I hope you are as well.
 I want to thank you all for the support you have given me over the years. I can't tell you how much all of the kind comments helped me going though some of my harder times. Thank you ❤️.
title: RLSL的未来

11月是我们推出Brave1.0的一周年纪念日，它是最私密、最安全、速度最快(3-6倍)的浏览器。在过去的一年里，我们在多个方面看到了惊人的增长，我们很高兴地庆祝Brave1.0的一周年，宣布我们的月度活跃用户(2050万)正式突破2000万，高于去年同期的870万。这意味着一年内增长了2.3倍。
Brave还突破了700万日活跃用户，高于去年同期的300万，显示DAU比去年同期增长了2.3倍。在过去的一年里，Brave收到了无数的好评，现在是Google Play中排名第一的浏览器。自从我们的iOS浏览器在去年9月成为iOS14苹果设备的默认选项以来，我们的iOS日活跃用户增长了34%。
Brave首席执行官兼联合创始人布兰登·艾奇(Brendan Eich)表示：“用户受够了监视资本主义，2000万人转向了Brave，建立了一个全新的网络生态系统，拥有可选的广告经济，让他们重新控制自己的浏览体验。”全球隐私运动正在获得牵引力，这一里程碑只是我们让默认隐私成为所有网络用户的标准之旅的又一步。“。
经过认证的创作者：目前有近100万经过认证的创作者和出版商(截至11月2日为97.2万人)，高于去年的30万人，12个月内增长了3.2倍。
广告商和广告活动：勇敢的广告活动目前在近200个国家和地区得到支持，迄今已有超过20亿次广告确认活动。已经有来自460多个广告商的2215个广告宣传活动，这些品牌来自Verizon、家得宝、纽约时报、进步保险、Newegg、Chipotle、Chess.com、Privacy.com、PayPal/Honey、Ledger、亚马逊、哈里的剃须刀、CBS、Kind零食、罗技、联想、GrubHub、Belkin、Quickbook和Evernote。Brave广告活动的平均点击率(点击率)为9%，远高于行业平均水平仅为2%，但许多品牌的参与度甚至更高。
向出版商和创作者捐赠BAT的数量：勇敢的用户奖励了他们最喜欢的出版商和创作者，向他们捐赠了2600万BAT。勇敢的用户可以通过在台式机和移动设备上激活Brave Rewards及其集成的Brave钱包来支持YouTube、Twitch、Twitter、GitHub等网站或创建者。这一功能允许用户发送BAT作为优秀内容的提示，要么直接在浏览时发送，要么默认按月付费。勇敢的用户通过选择观看保护隐私的勇敢广告(占广告收入份额的70%)来赚取BAT。
